How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-22 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Detects tickers flagged by two or more coherent screeners within one trading day; cross-screener overlap acts as a confirmation filter ranking validated combos first.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

MS leads at 164, hitting CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PULLBACK, and VARIANCE_PREMIUM, the cleanest setup in the batch; LLY at 131 replicates that exact trio, making it the natural runner-up on identical logic. AEHR at 114 stacks two PEAD signals plus PULLBACK, suggesting earnings-drift continuation with a technical reset. Score distribution is top-heavy, three names pulling clear of six bunched at exactly 100 and a tail at 94 and 92, not a broad or evenly spread batch. No ticker today matched a validated combo, and the NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.2 adds no directional tailwind, keeping conviction capped across the board. MS is the first call at low-to-moderate conviction, with a hard preference to wait for a validated-combo match before adding size.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: VLO (score 8, target ~$275.43 (DD20d -11.6%)).

2 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 1 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

🧠 Flags institutional options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta, premium size, EMA alignment, and low-OI anomalies to isolate smart money direction.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $XYZ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1XYZ ↑50 Watch$76.63 -1.07%BULLISH SWEEPCALL83$240KOPENING113958WEAK22
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The single resolved 90-day trade posts 200% return versus SPY at 2.1%, and monthly cohorts show 100% win rates from April through June with average 20-day returns ranging from 3,610% to 8,847%, but with only 1 resolved trade out of 237 live signals the numbers are statistically empty and almost certainly reflect open-position marks rather than closed trades, leaving the backtest Sharpe of 2.39 and 68.1% win rate across 273 trades as the only credible anchor.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (1 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 238 signals captured · 1 resolved at +20d · WR 100% · avg +200.0% · vs SPY +2.1%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 237 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single name, XYZ, scoring 50 in Watch tier on a BULLISH SWEEP pattern - below both the 65 and 80 score thresholds that would indicate meaningful conviction. Score distribution is as thin as it gets: one signal, median and max both at 50, zero names clearing the 65-plus or 80-plus cutoffs. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (49.2), which neither amplifies nor dampens the signal. At this score level and with a single low-tier sweep, XYZ carries no actionable edge today - pass unless the next intraday refresh shows score expansion above 65.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape confirms the backtest: 500 resolved trades show 77.2% WR and +3.73% avg return versus SPY's +0.58%, and monthly WR has climbed from 50.8% in April to 77.2% in June, with the +3.1 point edge over SPY being the number that matters.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

FLG at 100 and CAVA at 99 lead the batch, both Very Strong tier with no pattern overlay, so the setup rests purely on VRP alignment. INBX at 97 adds a biotech name worth watching for event risk given its smaller float. The score distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals print at 80 or above and the median sits at 75, making this a strong rather than thin trawl. NEUTRAL macro at 49.2 neither helps nor hurts, as VRP_MID carries no macro directional tilt built into its construction. Start with FLG at high conviction, size modestly until pattern data populates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Every signal in today's batch scores exactly 100 with a median of 100, a full ceiling that reads as a scoring artifact rather than a genuinely elite cohort. BUG is the most structurally interesting pick because elevated put flow on a sector ETF signals a broad-based hedge rather than a single-name view, which fits the Kerberos contrarian thesis more cleanly than a small-cap name does. CALX and CLDX are the single-stock names worth the first look among the eight listed, though pattern fields are null for all picks, leaving the specific triggering setup unresolved. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.2 is a non-event for this strategy, neither amplifying nor undermining the put-surge edge. With no internal differentiation and no pattern data, position sizing is guesswork, so treat this as a watchlist and wait for score dispersion at the next refresh before committing.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live data contradicts the backtest directly: 46% WR and avg -1.49% versus SPY +0.52% earns a KILL verdict, roughly 19 percentage points below the backtest's 65% WR, though the June 2026 monthly cohort at 60% WR and +2.11% avg suggests the tape may be slowly recovering.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
STARTER ONLYWR 46% · avg -1.5% vs SPY +0.5% (-2.0 pts)
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 491 resolved at +20d · WR 46% · avg -1.5% · vs SPY +0.5%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 495 closed · WR TB 38% · 15% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PENN leads with a perfect 100 score and Very strong tier, followed by ARKO at 91, both printing LAUNCH patterns. A five-name cluster at 86 rounds out the Strong tier: HTLD, MCS, MSGS, NEU, RBCAA. Score distribution is broadly elevated, all 50 signals clear 65 and 20 clear 80 with a median of 77, so this reads as a wide, decent-quality batch rather than thin or top-heavy. Neutral macro at 49.2 is tolerable but offers no tailwind for a reversal setup. Take PENN first, but at reduced conviction: live tracking shows Very strong tier names averaging -3.75% over 20 days versus -1.29% for Signal tier names, which cuts against the two highest-scoring picks.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax via VVIX percentile and VIX term structure to flag sell-vol opportunities when fear peaks at extremes.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑60 Watch$739.10 -1.11%SELL VOL1.25
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Backtest stats are absent (all fields null, rigorous=false), so the 90d tape is the only evidence base: 73.5% WR and a thin +0.1pt edge over SPY earns KEEP, but monthly cohorts show April's vol-spike conditions (100% WR, +5.4% avg on 4 names) inflating the aggregate while May and June compressed to sub-1% avg returns, meaning the live edge concentrates almost entirely in Signal-tier names at 91% WR and +1.97% avg, which is the number that matters.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 2.33 [CI 0.69-4.21] · N=72 · WR 61% · PF 2.16 · p(Bonf) 0.097 · ½-Kelly 0.16 Full methodology →
🧠 Catches informed positioning by flagging calm, non-extended stocks where options volume exceeds 5x the 20-day mean with premium above $50K.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.
Top picks: $UVXY $BKD $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikeDTEPremiumVol/OIIVDeltaTimingFlowOIToxicity
1UVXY ↑65● Signal$25.78 +8.50%BEARISH SWEEPPUT24.57$171K104.7084.5%-0.30CONTRARIAN230.45
2BKD ↑61 Watch$14.72 +0.51%BULLISH SWEEPCALL17147$701K91.0047.2%0.37EARLY550.42
3SPY ↑59 Watch$739.10 -1.11%BEARISH SWEEPPUT7336$793K11.6011.0%-0.24EARLY5x REPEAT3290.43
4SOXS ↑53 Watch$45.20 +1.53%BULLISH SWEEPCALL5214$424K39.20211.7%0.46EARLY210.49
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Monthly cohorts from March through June show 100% win rates across 1,616 total signals, superficially echoing the 90-day window's 100% print, but with only 14 resolved trades against a 20-trade minimum the live tape is too thin to confirm or challenge the backtest's 61.1% win rate and marginal Bonferroni p of 0.097.

GDX prints the only other bullish sweep at 68, while SPY, SOXX, and IWM show bearish sweeps tagged LONG alongside TLT's hedge pattern, a cluster that reads as institutional hedging rather than fresh directional bets. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (49.2), offering no directional tailwind to the batch. Preference: DRAM first at high conviction on the score gap; skip the ETF hedges as actionable longs.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

XLRE is the only name with conviction today, scoring 95 (Very strong) while every other pick sits at Watch or below. XLV and XLP at 60 are the next-best but neither clears the 65 threshold that marks meaningful coverage. The distribution is top-heavy with a floor cluster, as 5 of the 8 listed names score exactly 50 and only 2 of 11 total signals reach 80. With macro at NEUTRAL (49.2), there is no regime tailwind supporting sector rotation, and pattern labels are absent across the board, leaving every setup without a named catalyst. XLRE is the only actionable name today, at moderate conviction given the flat macro backdrop and the thin bench behind it.

🧠 Flags US equities off 40%-plus from 52-week highs with a capitulation volume spike and reversal candle, targeting mean reversion off forced-seller exhaustion.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape at 56% WR and +4.1% avg beats SPY by 3.9 points, broadly confirming real edge, but the critical contradiction is the tier inversion: Very strong names run at 40% WR and -2.75% avg while Signal-tier names run at 60.7% WR and +5.3% avg, which directly undermines the scoring model's rank-ordering and is the single most important number in this dataset.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ENLV and FIGS both print 100 scores in the Very strong tier with a BREAKOUT pattern, joined by CMTL and VRRM at 95 and ALEC at 93, but live tracking reveals a damaging tier inversion: Very strong names average -2.75% over 20 days at only 40% WR in the 90-day window, the worst cohort in the system. The batch is clustered high, with 32 of 50 signals at or above 80 and a median of 81, which looks productive on the surface but maps directly to the underperforming live tier. NEUTRAL macro at 49.2 neither supports nor penalizes the setup. Given the tier inversion, ALEC at 93 or any lower-tier name not surfaced here represents a better risk-adjusted entry than the perfect-score pair, and overall conviction stays low until Very strong live performance stabilizes.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks stocks by annualized exponential-regression slope times R-squared over 90 days, buying only clean trends above MA100 when SPY clears MA200.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

HUM at score 100 is the only name that clears the Very strong bar, and it is the sole entry above 80 in a batch of 16 signals. PANW at 73 is the second-best name but sits in Signal tier, not Very strong, and every other pick lands in Watch with scores at 25 or below. The distribution is lopsided: median score of 8 means the majority of this batch is barely registering, not a healthy spread. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (49.2), which leaves the SPY-MA200 regime gate ambiguous and argues for reduced sizing across the board. Start with HUM at moderate conviction only, treat PANW as a distant second, and skip everything else.

🧠 Detects stocks holding post-earnings gaps after a positive EPS surprise, capturing delayed price discovery as analysts revise and momentum extends.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $AMBP $ANGO $AIR

7BLK ↑55 Watch$1047.16 -0.90%9.6%4-3.4%

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (55.3% win rate, avg +3.5%, beating SPY by 3.1 pts) nominally confirms an edge above the MARGINAL backtest verdict, but the June 2026 cohort crashed to 37% win rate and -0.65% average, a recent break that contradicts the aggregate trend and warrants size caution going into this batch.

🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more intermarket pairs each break two-sigma correlation in seven days, betting on regime mean reversion.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape at 69% win rate and +2.5% average outperforming SPY's -0.6% by 3.0 points lends observational support to the informal Sharpe 3.4 noted in the construction source, while monthly cohort averages from April through June remain consistently positive (April +8.4%, May +2.5%, June +4.0%) even as cohort win rates of 52-59% trail the 90-day headline, suggesting the live edge is real but the 69% figure overstates the near-term run rate.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

FBIZ leads at a score of 96 and CWAN follows at 93, both Very strong tier, with DHR at 91 rounding out the top three candidates worth prioritizing. All eight picks carry LONG direction and null pattern fields, meaning the signal is pure premium size and OI confirmation with no additional structural read available. The score distribution is top-heavy: median 78 across 50 signals with 20 names clearing 80, a tight upper cluster that is unusual for a 50-name batch and reflects genuine selectivity today. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.2 provides no tailwind, so the edge here is name-specific rather than regime-driven, which raises the bar for sizing. Lead with FBIZ at high conviction, anchored by the 96 score and the Very strong tier's 88.9% historical win rate and 8.5% average return in the 90-day tape.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch of 50 signals is analytically inert: every single name scores 100, median 100, zero spread across the distribution, so the screener provides no internal ranking to work with. AAON and AEIS are the most operationally grounded names on the list, both carrying industrial and semiconductor-equipment exposure that fits a re-acceleration read, but with identical scores and no patterns flagged for any ticker, that preference is texture rather than signal. A completely maxed-out score distribution almost always means the filter is casting too wide a net or the scoring model is saturating, and a NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.2 adds no directional lift. If forced to act, AAON first, half-size, given sector, with the explicit acknowledgment that the screener itself is offering no edge today.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the screener earns its KEEP verdict - WR of 57.6% and avg return of +2.0% beats SPY by 2.1 points - but the key number is the Strong tier's 73.0% WR and +8.9% avg versus the Very Strong tier's brutal 15.8% WR and -12.9% avg, a split that the monthly cohort improvement from April's -0.73% to May/June's +4.2% avg does nothing to resolve and which makes tier placement more important than raw score.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AKR, BV, and DFH lead today at the maximum score of 85, all in the Strong tier with a PRIMED pattern, making them the clearest starting point. ABEO, ABX, AMCX, ASAN, and BCAX cluster just behind at 80, also Strong tier. The 50-signal batch is top-heavy: 12 names score 80 or above out of 50, median lands at 70, so the field thins quickly below that top cohort. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.2 provides no directional tailwind, which matters in a screener that depends on market-wide risk appetite to convert short pressure into actual covering. Prefer AKR as the first look given its tie for the top score and the historical edge concentrated in the Strong tier, sized modestly given the neutral tape.

🧠 Ranks stocks by relative volume times multi-horizon price momentum to identify breakout leaders with accelerating institutional sponsorship.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is nearly impossible to differentiate: all 50 signals score 80 or above, the median is 96, and four names (ARWR, BIPC, TDY, WAB) sit in a four-way tie at 100 with no pattern data to break the deadlock. RGEN and RRC at 99 carry the same problem. A score distribution this compressed into the top of the range means the screen is flagging everyone as a leader, which is functionally the same as flagging nobody. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 49.2, providing no directional justification for pressing a momentum tilt. Given the KILL tracking verdict, any long from this batch is low-conviction - if forced to act, ARWR first given biotech volume tends to resolve faster, but size it small.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects rejection tails of at least 1.8x ATR at Market Profile value-area extremes, flagging exhaustion-driven mean reversion on daily bars.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $RPHS
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1RPHS ↑90●●● Very strong$8.79 -0.28%LOW1.8126.8%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape contradicts the backtest's +3.18% mean - 10 resolved trades show a 50% win rate and a -0.54% avg 20-day return versus SPY at +1.84%, and May's 67% win rate has already faded back to 50% in June, with the sample too small at 10 resolved to call anything definitive.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single name. RPHS scores 90, earns a Very strong long tier, and carries a null pattern field, meaning the setup is a raw tail-location hit without additional confirmation. Score distribution is trivially thin - max and median both sit at 90 with only one qualifying signal. Macro is NEUTRAL at 49.2, providing no directional tailwind. Take RPHS at minimum size only - one signal on a neutral tape is a watch, not a chase.

🧠 Identifies the first weekly close above the prior-week high after a post-earnings red-candle pullback, targeting PEAD momentum continuation.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%
  • Volume confirmation: gap-day volume / 20d average > 1.5
  • Dollar-volume ≥ $50M
Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. 🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW signals by skipping trades where KVO or Force Index flips are active within 3 days, using volume anomalies as a negative screen to isolate cleaner institutional flow.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch runs 38 signals with a median score of 42 and only 4 names at 80 or above, so quality is thin outside the top few. ALMS (100) and STTK (99) headline the batch alongside AMDL (96), all three in the Very Strong tier, but that tier is the worst performer in live tracking, posting a 25% win rate and -17% average return across 415 resolved trades. LQDA (77) and ORKA (70) sit in the Signal tier, which is the only tier showing a positive live average at +6.6% and a 62.5% win rate, albeit over just 16 trades. Score distribution is top-heavy and collapses fast, with the median at 42 confirming that the bulk of today's batch is noise rather than actionable signal. NEUTRAL macro at 49.2 provides no regime tailwind for breakouts, and given the KILL verdict on the screener, the only defensible play is a speculative small long in LQDA as the single Signal-tier name with the best live analog, at lowest conviction.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

The score distribution makes this batch useless for differentiation - all 50 signals print between 99 and 100 (median 99), meaning the model is producing a degenerate, ceiling-compressed output where no name ranks above any other. AMD, MU, and DELL sit nominally at the top with scores of 100, but all pattern fields are null, stripping out any structural reason to prefer one over the others in a batch where every name looks identical. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 49.2, which neither confirms nor blocks a long bias. The backtest carries a REJECT verdict - headline returns were a leveraged-ETF artifact and true alpha was -0.8%/yr at p=0.25, meaning this model captures beta, not edge. Conviction is zero; pass on this batch entirely.

Data confidence STARTER · edge unproven validity test Sharpe 0.98 N=8 CI extreme, doctrine-rejected solo Full methodology →
🧠 Weekly RSI(14) below 30 gates entry; daily RSI crossover, candlestick reversal, and zone scoring rank contrarian mean-reversion setups.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $ABTC $NFLX $ASR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSI (D)RSI (W)CrossoverPattern
1ABTC ↑58 Watch$6.30 +3.28%34.4327.76YESENGULFING
2NFLX ↑58 Watch$68.86 +0.48%19.6312.07NOHAMMER
3ASR ↑55 Watch$271.25 -0.83%19.9427.15NOHAMMER
4AA ↑54 Watch$45.66 -1.11%44.8226.25YESDRAGONFLY
5CINT ↑52 Watch$3.18 +1.27%15.4529.62NOENGULFING
6ENLV ↑50 Watch$4.45 -2.94%6.8813.64NOENGULFING
7TLRY ↑50 Watch$4.05 -2.52%19.0516.32NOHAMMER
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking at 54% WR and +3.5% avg beats SPY by 2.5 points (verdict KEEP), which is the only real evidence here since the backtest is doctrine-rejected at N=8 with no valid Sharpe, though the May cohort's avg of -0.44% on a 56% WR is a warning that fat left tails can gut realized alpha in risk-off months.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ALLO leads at 68, the only name with real separation from the pack, Signal tier with a SETUP pattern that checks all three layers of the filter. ASR at 65 is the only other Signal-tier name and sits right at the threshold, also flagged SETUP rather than just WATCHLIST. CCJ at 60 earns a mention as a Watch-tier SETUP in uranium, a sector with its own idiosyncratic catalyst risk on both sides. The score distribution is thin and compressed: median 52, max 68, zero names above 80, only 2 clearing 65, which tells you today's batch lacks conviction at the top of the range. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 49.2, offering no directional tailwind but no active headwind either - take ALLO first at low-to-moderate conviction, hold off on anything below 60 until the tape gives more.

🧠 Detects unusual volume, OI delta, and premium shifts in crypto-linked ETFs to front-run directional moves in bitcoin miners and spot-crypto proxies.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $MARA $IREN
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1MARA ↑60 Watch$12.71 +2.46%44.12-21.3%risk_on
2IREN ↑55 Watch$42.16 +2.13%46.99-30.3%risk_on
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape confirms the REJECT verdict without ambiguity: 90-day win rate of 29% at an average return of -9.7% against SPY's +0.2%, and the by-tier breakdown shows the damage concentrates exactly where you do not want it - the 'Very strong' cohort posts 0% wins with a -34.9% average, meaning the screener's highest-conviction signals lose the hardest, and June 2026 collapsed to 0% wins at -19.9% average, accelerating sharply from May's thin +0.8% mean.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XOP tops the batch at 72, the only name with meaningful separation from the field, followed by HACK at 67 and ILF at 66 - cybersecurity and Latin America exposure rounding out the top three alongside energy. The distribution is weak: median sits at 54, no name clears 80, and only 5 of 50 clear 65, making this a flat, low-conviction batch with little top-end density. All pattern fields are null across every pick, stripping out any technical confirmation and leaving raw score as the only differentiator. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.2 offers no tailwind for theme rotation, and the clustering of Watch-tier names between 60 and 62 adds noise without adding quality. Take XOP first on score leadership alone, small size, and hold the Watch tier until pattern data populates.

FULL SIZE OKWR 65% · avg +7.9% · beats SPY -0.9% by +8.8 pts
Last 90 days: 136 signals captured · 131 resolved at +20d · WR 65% · avg +7.9% · vs SPY -0.9%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 136 closed · WR TB 37% · 35% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 8 top picks score 100 at Very strong tier with pattern field null, so the screener gives no internal ranking between them. BL and CELH are the growth reads to prioritize, SRTY is an inverse small-cap instrument flagged LONG (the standout oddity in this batch), and HDV provides the defensive read if you want one. Score distribution is concentrated in the 65-79 band (29 of 50 signals), thin from 80-99 (4 signals), and spikes at 100 (8 signals), making today's top picks the ceiling cohort by construction. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.2 provides no tailwind, and without pattern data across the board, structural conviction is low. First preference is CELH at low conviction, with SRTY held as a watch until the logic for flagging a short-exposure instrument LONG is confirmed.

🧠 Detects hedge fund consensus via quarterly 13F scoring: new positions score highest, exits subtract, surfacing names multiple whales are accumulating simultaneously.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 Identifies lagging individual stocks within LATE_CHASE and RIDING sectors, scored by sector relative strength multiplied by individual stock underperformance.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch is uniformly weak: all 21 signals sit at score 50, the floor, with nothing above 65 and no pattern data on any name. SBAC, CSGP, and BXMT are the most recognizable names, but each lands at the same Watch tier score of 50 with null patterns, so there is no basis to rank one over another. The score distribution is not clustered or top-heavy, it is completely flat, which signals the screener found sector laggards but the price divergence required to generate real conviction is not present. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (49.2), meaning there is no regime tailwind to accelerate catch-up rotation into these names. Pass the entire batch today, zero conviction across the board.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks US equities by EV/EBIT ascending to surface the cheapest operating-earnings names, exploiting the market's systematic overpricing of growth relative to value.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $BZ $WRD $KROS

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (57% win rate, avg +5.1% over 21 resolved names, beating SPY +0.6% by +4.5 points, verdict KEEP) and the sole June 2026 cohort show identical numbers, confirming the signal is consistent, though no formal backtest yet anchors whether that +4.5-point spread survives a statistically rigorous test.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Detects coordinated insider accumulation via Form 4 cluster buys, weighted by rank and dollar size, on the thesis that 3+ insiders buying together signals conviction.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.
Top picks: $CLBK $CTM $FNWD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayBuysCXOClusterAvg value
1CLBK ↑60 Watch$10.88 -0.59%16✓✓$299K
2CTM ↑60 Watch$0.66 +3.65%4✓✓$1K
3FNWD ↑60 Watch$36.49 -0.37%6✓✓$1K
4GLOO ↑60 Watch$3.30 -2.94%4✓✓$2250K
5SWZ ↑60 Watch$5.91 +0.00%4✓✓$25K
6TSM ↑60 Watch$417.64 -0.85%36✓✓$26K
7WBX ↑60 Watch$4.12 -0.72%4✓✓$1245K
8YORW ↑60 Watch$31.14 +0.26%7✓✓$2K
9COE ↑50 Watch$15.80 -4.24%6✓·$13659K
10PRQR ↑50 Watch$1.80 -0.28%4✓·$247K
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No rigorous backtest exists yet, so the only quality signal is the live 90d tape, where a 61% win rate and avg return of +5.3% beat SPY by 5.5 points, though the monthly cohort WR slipping from 64% in May to 57.5% in June marks a deceleration worth monitoring.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Surfaces momentum leaders by ranking tickers on 20-day relative strength vs SPY within bullish sectors, weighted by sector breadth and cluster position.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $ABX $ACA $ACAD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1ABX ↑80●● Strong$10.10 -1.94%Financials18.7%30.6%
2ACA ↑68● Signal$144.84 -0.20%Industrials12.2%27.2%
3ACAD ↑64 Watch$24.82 -0.56%Healthcare18.9%13.9%
4ABVX ↑58 Watch$129.44 -0.22%Healthcare40.6%7.9%
5ACCO ↑57 Watch$3.96 -2.73%Industrials-2.5%25.5%
6AAMI ↑55 Watch$81.07 -1.52%Financials0.8%21.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.

STARTER · untested

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices Yang-Zhang HV20, exploiting the tendency for realized vol to exceed implied vol ahead of events.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $XOM $LYFT $ROKU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1XOM ↑65● Signal$157.91 +2.24%PRE_IV_RAMP8—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2LYFT ↑50 Watch$13.81 -5.78%PRE_IV_RAMP14—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
3ROKU ↑50 Watch$142.57 -0.36%PRE_IV_RAMP7—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
4RTX ↓50 Watch$209.04 +7.27%REVERSAL0DOWN (reversal)BUY PUT (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

CVX, PG, and XOM all land at score 65, the floor of Signal tier, with no pattern data attached to any name. CVX and XOM are both energy, so a vol buyer taking both carries correlated sector risk around the same earnings window. PG is the cleaner isolated bet and the more defensible choice in a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 49.2), where neither risk appetite nor vol expectations are running hot. The score distribution is flat and clustered: four signals, median and max both at 65, zero names clearing 80. ROKU sits at 50 on Watch and adds nothing urgent. First preference is PG straddle, low conviction given all scores are bunched at the tier floor and no pattern confirmation is available for any name.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects tickers where 30-day implied vol spikes more than 1.5 sigma above 20-day realized vol, flagging candidates for premium selling.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $BMRN $IWM $BNTX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1BMRN ↓83●● Strong$58.63 +0.40%49%23%2.09EXTREME_FEAR
2IWM ↓70● Signal$292.15 -0.56%27%15%1.76FEAR
3BNTX ↓66● Signal$91.88 +0.05%45%27%1.67FEAR
4ALNY ↓62 Watch$265.42 -0.59%58%37%1.56FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape contradicts any confidence here: a win rate of 41.6% and average gain of 0.9% lag SPY's 1.1%, the monthly cohort trend has deteriorated sharply from April's 55.9% win rate and +10.8% average down to June's 30.3% win rate and -3.8% average, and the backtest remains unvalidated at N=25, short of the required 30 resolved trades, so there is no confirmed edge to lean on.

HALF SIZEWR 42% · avg +0.9% ≈ SPY +1.1% (-0.2 pts)

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags retail euphoria via social attention z-score, call/put skew, and parabolic price with no earnings catalyst, generating short candidates.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $CCI $ABG $ARWR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1CCI ↓100●●● Very strong$77.19 -0.40%FOMO1.751.7%
2ABG ↓60 Watch$220.28 -1.38%GREED0.603.4%
3ARWR ↓56 Watch$88.08 -0.70%GREED0.5619.0%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects value area false breaks: a close outside VAL or VAH followed by next-day reclaim signals trapped shorts or longs and a squeeze.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AR $AGCO $AVBP
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AR ↑75● Signal$35.73 +1.32%BEAR_TRAP34.1236.411.04
2AGCO ↑73● Signal$119.67 +2.04%BEAR_TRAP111.45117.793.65
3AVBP ↑70● Signal$30.86 +1.38%BEAR_TRAP27.4530.571.97
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No backtest exists yet (N=0, needs 30 resolved trades), so the 90-day live tape is the only validity anchor: WR 65% and avg +4.9% vs. SPY +0.1% is constructive, but the monthly cohorts show deceleration from May (WR 77%, avg +8.0%) to June (WR 63%, avg +4.4%), and the key number to watch is whether July holds above that 63% floor before treating this screener as confirmed.

Construction
Composite ranking of HRHR-style options trades: combines best confluence (e.g. SMART_FLOW × UNUSUAL_V3), Sharpe/WR of the combo, IV rank, IV/HV spread, days-to-earnings, 52w position, regime multiplier.

🧠 Identifies stocks that break above the Value Area High intraday then close back inside, trapping bulls and triggering a short signal.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $AHCO $ACGL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1AHCO ↓65● Signal$10.60 -2.57%BULL_TRAP11.479.96
2ACGL ↓55 Watch$99.49 +0.87%BULL_TRAP101.0294.09
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where put IV far exceeds call IV above the 90th percentile, detecting unusual one-sided put demand as a potential directional signal.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $LRCX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1LRCX ↑66● Signal$322.89 +1.13%5.6397.1%100BEAR_SKEW_MILD
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No completed backtest exists to confirm or contradict, but the 90-day live record (9 resolved trades, 55.6% win rate, mean -5.70% versus SPY's +0.77%) combined with the swing from May's +14.6% average down to June's -24.5% shows month-to-month noise is dominating any edge, and the key number is the 'Strong' tier's two trades averaging -55.9%, which single-handedly drags the overall mean negative and flags tier labeling as unreliable at this sample size.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put/call skew spikes vs the 90-day baseline and fades the hedging crowd when the underlying is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $TSLA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1TSLA ↓100●●● Very strong$323.98 -13.38%298%97—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 51%, avg +4.0%, beats SPY by 3.1 pts) masks a damning tier inversion, Watch outperforming Very strong by 35.8 percentage points in win rate and 14.8 points in average return, and the June cohort's -7.1% average against May's +10.1% confirms the screener's recent edge is degrading, the key number being Watch's +9.8% vs Very strong's -5.0%.

🧠 VOL_EDGE_R1 detects volatility mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and options flow composite.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $SVXY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1SVXY ↓85●● Strong$56.24 -2.68%24%819%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms a broken screener: 24% win rate and an avg return of -8.8% against SPY at -0.4%, a gap of -8.3 points, with all three monthly cohorts in the red (April worst at -24.6%), leaving no statistical case to override the KILL verdict.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
STARTER ONLYWR 24% · avg -8.8% vs SPY -0.4% (-8.3 pts)
Last 90 days: 51 signals captured · 29 resolved at +20d · WR 24% · avg -8.8% · vs SPY -0.4%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 30 closed · WR TB 23% · 7% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Screens equity universe for financial distress risk using Altman Z-Score balance sheet ratios, flagging candidates near default thresholds.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA flags price-spread and volume divergence patterns where narrow spreads on high volume or wide spreads on low volume signal institutional absorption or distribution.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.

No actionable preference exists from this output; sit on hands.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
